title: Files should avoid having too many lines of code
description:
Files should not have too many lines of codeAnalyze your codeadaptability-focusedmaintainabilityCode Smellbrain-overloadWhen a source file grows too much, it can accumulate numerous responsibilities and become challenging to understand and maintain. Above a specific threshold, refactor the file into smaller files whose code focuses on well-defined tasks.  Those smaller files will be easier to
understand and test. Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: File has too many lines ({line_count}) - consider breaking into smaller files (recommended: <300 lines)
File is excessively large ({line_count} lines) - must be refactored into smaller, focused modules
File content exceeds recommended size - consider splitting into multiple focused modules for better maintainability
